journal_entry.description: Discounted 90-day non-interest-bearing note at bank (50 days after issue)
journal_entry.rationale: Ford discounts a $100,000 note receivable at the bank with 40 days remaining. The bank charges a discount of $1,666.67 (15% annual rate × $100,000 × 40/365 days). Ford receives cash of $98,333.33, removes the note receivable, and recognizes the discount charge as a financing expense (interest expense).

clarification_questions: Note discounting is a financing transaction: cash increases (asset), interest expense increases (cost of early cash), note receivable decreases (asset). Discount = $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,666.67. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,666.67 = $98,333.33.
